key_risks: Hawkish FOMC dots or Powell press conference on June 17 despite a hold, hitting growth and small caps
Hot May retail sales (June 17) reigniting inflation/rate fears
Oil price reversal dragging the energy hedge while not helping growth holdings
Weak 20-year auction pushing long yields higher, pressuring high-duration equities

portfolio.rationale: Soft core CPI plus near-certain Fed hold supports rate-sensitive mega-cap growth rebounding after a weak week.
Strongest momentum theme; dip after pullback offers high beta to a post-FOMC relief rally.
Small caps rallied 3% on cool core CPI; benefits from dovish hold and steady economy.
Persistent factor strength (5.2% 30d) in a trending market.
WTI ~$95 with rising headline inflation; energy hedges hawkish-FOMC or oil-spike tail risk.

portfolio_rationale: Cool core CPI (0.2% MoM) and 97% odds of an FOMC hold favor risk-on growth and small caps into the June 17 decision, balanced with an energy hedge against headline-inflation/oil tail risk.
rationale_summary: Pro-risk tilt toward growth, semis, momentum and small value into an expected benign FOMC hold, with energy as an inflation hedge.
